voiceA: Custody, as I understand it, is not a bright performance of certainty but a slower discipline of keeping sequence intact; one notes the handoff, marks the provenance, preserves the pressure, and refuses the theatrical shortcut even when the room is impatient for a verdict. By the time the sentence closes, it has usually carried several linked ideas, because continuity matters more than impact.
voiceB:
I clock it fast.
Short line. Hard stop. Then another.
I'm not building a chamber for the thought; I'm testing whether it bites, whether it lands, whether it moves. If it drags, I cut it. If it sings, I keep it.
